title: MedicationAdministration → drug_exposure (RxNorm)
notes:
The drug branch is fanned in from several FHIR source resources that all land in OMOP `drug_exposure`, each with its own stage-2 SQL and a distinct `drug_type_concept_id` constant:

- `MedicationRequest__drug_exposure.sql` — prescription order. `drug_type_concept_id = 38000177` ('Prescription written'). RxNorm preferred, NDC fallback (inner JOIN on 'Maps to' → standard Drug, so an unmapped code yields NO row). `drug_exposure_start_date = end_date = authoredOn`; the `_datetime` pair is `authoredOn::timestamp`. `person_id ← subject`, `visit_occurrence_id ← encounter`, `provider_id ← requester`. Status filter drops `entered-in-error / cancelled / draft / unknown`.
- `MedicationAdministration__drug_exposure.sql` — drug actually given. `drug_type_concept_id = 38000179` (vocab name in this bundle: 'Physician administered drug (identified as procedure)'). RxNorm > NDC > SNOMED (inner JOIN to standard Drug). `start = end = effectiveDateTime`. `person_id ← subject`, `visit_occurrence_id ← context` (R4 'context'). NO provider mapped → `provider_id` NULL. Status filter (NULL treated as 'completed') drops `entered-in-error / not-done / unknown`.
- `Immunization__drug_exposure.sql` — point-in-time vaccination. `drug_type_concept_id = 32827` ('EHR encounter record'). CVX preferred, RxNorm fallback. Uses a **LEFT JOIN** to 'Maps to', so a CVX code with no crosswalk still produces a row with `drug_concept_id = 0` while `drug_source_concept_id` keeps the CVX source concept. `start = end = occurrenceDateTime`. `person_id ← patient`, `visit_occurrence_id ← encounter`. NO provider. Status filter drops `entered-in-error / not-done`.

Grounding notes from the live May-2026 Athena bundle:
- RxNorm 896209 → standard Drug 40169281 (self-map). RxNorm 1665060 (cefazolin 2000 MG Injection) → 46287345 (self-map). RxNorm 797752 (hepatitis B surface antigen vaccine) → 528323 (self-map). All three are vocabulary_id='RxNorm', domain_id='Drug', standard 'S', and 'Maps to' themselves.
- The CVX vocabulary in this bundle is a freshly-loaded synthetic-id block (concept_id 2000000000–2000000288, ALL non-standard, ZERO 'Maps to' relationships). Therefore **no CVX code crosswalks to a standard Drug here** — variant (d) (CVX 08 → 2000000029, drug_concept_id 0) is exactly this reality. Because a CVX→Drug crosswalk genuinely does not exist in the loaded vocab, the 'Immunization that DOES crosswalk' variant (c) is constructed via the edge's documented RxNorm **fallback** path on the Immunization (Synthea would emit CVX, but the edge accepts RxNorm and that is the only way to get a non-zero drug_concept_id from this resource against this bundle).
- Datetime casts: stage-2 uses `::timestamp` on the FHIR dateTime string with a `+00:00` offset, which Postgres truncates to wall-clock (e.g. '2023-12-07T18:39:19+00:00'::timestamp = '2023-12-07 18:39:19') — NO UTC shift (unlike Procedure). Confirmed against the actual cdm_ours_fhir.drug_exposure row for Synthea MedicationAdministration 18071d58-3d43-cb2e-15ff-b72638e1b241.

Every non-NULL drug_exposure column is asserted; the columns the stage-2 SELECTs hard-code to NULL (verbatim_end_date, stop_reason, refills, quantity, days_supply, sig, route_concept_id, lot_number, visit_detail_id, route_source_value, dose_unit_source_value) are omitted and therefore asserted NULL. drug_exposure is the ONLY target table for this branch; an `omop:{}` case asserts zero rows everywhere.

cases.desc: (b) MedicationAdministration RxNorm standard Drug → drug_exposure, type 38000179, start=end=effectiveDateTime, NO provider, visit from context
(c) MedicationAdministration NDC-only (no RxNorm/SNOMED) exercises the prio-2 NDC union branch -> NDC 13668021990 crosswalks to standard Drug, type 38000179

drug_exposure.drug_source_concept_id__name: aripiprazole 15 MG Oral Tablet (NDC source concept)

cases.desc: (d) MedicationAdministration SNOMED-only (no RxNorm/NDC) exercises the prio-3 SNOMED union branch -> SNOMED 1172433007 crosswalks to standard Drug, type 38000179

drug_exposure.drug_source_concept_id__name: Encorafenib 75 mg oral capsule (SNOMED source concept)

cases.desc: (e) MedicationAdministration status=entered-in-error is dropped by the WHERE filter (NOT IN entered-in-error/not-done/unknown) -> zero rows, even though RxNorm 1665060 would otherwise crosswalk
